id.description: Storage namespace prefix for persistent stores (locale/theme/fav/etc.).

theme.description: Default DaisyUI theme name used when no persisted theme exists (e.g. 'dim', 'light').

category.description: Store category — the launcher sections and filter-chips group apps by this.

version.description: App version (semver-ish) shown in the profile footer alongside the runtime core version. Defaults to 1.0.0.

translate.description: Item field names whose English body text is machine-translated to the active locale (via the runtime's cached, fail-open translator). Use for API content that isn't localized at source, e.g. Hacker News titles: ["title"]. Chrome/UI strings must still be authored in the i18n dicts. May include an enrich.body virtual field to translate fetched previews too.

localized.description: The data adapter already returns content in the ACTIVE locale (e.g. wiki fetching ${lang}.wikipedia.org), so body prose needs no runtime translation. Exempts the app from the card.body-must-be-translated rule. Do not set it just to silence the rule.

enrich.description: Article-preview enrichment for link feeds whose API has no preview text (e.g. Hacker News). The runtime fetches each item's URL description once (cached, fail-open) and exposes it as the virtual field named by `body` — point card.body (and optionally detail rows / translate) at that same field name.

url.description: Item field holding the outbound article URL to fetch a preview for.

body.description: Virtual field name the fetched description is exposed as (use it in card.body). Not a real item key.

fav.description: Enables favorites/bookmark feature. When present, at least one tab should set source:'fav'.

key.description: Item field name used as the unique favorites key. MUST be a non-empty field present on every item.

icon.description: Iconify icon for the profile avatar.

permissions.description: Show a history-backed Permissions screen (a row in the profile) with a toggle per listed permission; enabling triggers the native prompt.

label.description: i18n key for the row label (display uses fixed 'profSource', but kept for documentation).

url.description: External URL opened in a new tab.

icon.description: Iconify icon for the source row.

detail.description: In-app drill-down page. When present, tapping a list card opens a full-screen detail overlay (instead of card.href). All slot fields are item keys unless noted; row/action labels are i18n keys. Needs i18n key 'back'.

image.description: OPTIONAL item field — hero image URL (16:9).

title.description: REQUIRED item field — page + app-bar title.

subtitle.description: OPTIONAL item field — under the title.

body.description: OPTIONAL item field — long-form prose (the full description/summary), rendered as a readable paragraph block above the fact rows. The card can only show a 2-line clamp; this is where the full text goes. translate/enrich aware.

rows.description: Fact rows; a row whose field resolves empty/null is dropped.

label.description: i18n key for the row label.

field.description: Item field for the row value (pre-joined to a string in data.js).

format.description: Optional: locale-format the field as a date instead of raw text — 'ago' (past relative) or 'when' (future absolute+countdown from a timestamp).

actions.description: Action buttons at the bottom of the detail page. Each is EITHER an outbound link (href) OR an in-app video (play) — exactly one.

items.$comment: Exactly one of href/play. Both would be an action with two meanings — leave the app, or stay in it — and the runtime would have to guess which the author meant. Mirrored in packages/runtime/validate.js; the two validators must stay in lockstep.

icon.description: Iconify icon for the action button.

label.description: i18n key for the action label.

href.description: Item field holding the URL (opened in a new tab; sanitized).

play.description: Item field holding a video URL (HLS or progressive). Opens the runtime's full-screen player IN-APP: screen stays awake, picture-in-picture, fullscreen, and playback resumes where you left it (position kept by the runtime, keyed on the item). The player is history-backed — system Back closes it and returns to the detail.

filters.description: Filter sheet definition. Presence renders the filter button + bottom sheet.

defaults.description: Initial filter state. Keys SHOULD match controls[].key.

refetch.description: If true, pressing Apply re-runs data.load(filters).

controls.description: Filter controls rendered in the sheet.

type.description: Control kind. range = numeric from–to on a `field`; multi = multi-select chips (filter state is an array of chosen `options` values; for list tabs with a `field` it keeps items whose field is in the set).

key.description: Filter state key written into S.filters; referenced by clientFilters[].key and section.labelParams.

label.description: i18n key for the control label.

field.description: For type:'range' — item numeric field the from–to bounds filter.

unit.description: For type:'range' — unit shown by the label (e.g. 'BTC').

icon.description: Iconify icon shown beside the control.

iconCls.description: Optional extra CSS classes for the toggle icon.

optionsFrom.description: For type:'select' — meta key on data.meta holding [{v,l},...] options. The data loader MUST populate meta[optionsFrom].

options.description: Inline [value, labelKey] pairs (localized via T). Required for 'segment' and 'multi'; an alternative to 'optionsFrom' for 'select'.

prefixItems.description: Option value written into filter state.
i18n key (or literal) for the option label.

refetch.description: Documents that changing this control should trigger a refetch (filters.refetch drives actual reload on Apply).

chip.description: If true and the filter is active, show a removable chip above the list.

tabs.description: Bottom-dock tabs. Must contain at least one tab; tabs[0] is the initial tab.

type.description: Renderer: list -> ListView, converter -> ConverterView, profile -> Profile, dashboard -> DashboardView, tool -> a custom app-supplied view (sensor/instrument apps).

view.description: tool only — key into the views map passed to start(spec, { views }); names the app's custom Preact component (the irreducible interactive visual).

needs.description: tool only (OPTIONAL) — hardware capabilities the view uses (e.g. ['haptic','geo','camera']); documents intent / drives permission priming.

icon.description: Iconify icon for the dock button.

label.description: i18n key for the dock label.

titleKey.description: i18n key for the app-bar title when this tab is active (defaults to 'title').

search.description: list only — show the search/status bar.

paginate.description: list only — infinite scroll. data.js load() receives filters.cursor and returns { items, meta, next } where `next` is an opaque cursor for the following page (null/omitted = no more). The runtime appends pages via an accessible 'load more' sentinel (button + IntersectionObserver). Ignored on the saved/fav tab. Needs i18n key 'loadMore'.

searchFetch.description: list only (search family) — the search box debounce-drives a real refetch: the trimmed query reaches data.js load() as filters.q, and client-side filtering is skipped (the server already searched). Empty query shows the `prompt` empty-state unless `browse` is set. Requires search:true.

browse.description: list only, with searchFetch — an empty query LOADS instead of prompting. A catalogue is browsed first and searched second (popular packages, top films): demanding a query before showing anything is right for a search box (wiki) and wrong for a shelf. data.js load() must handle an empty filters.q. Without it, searchFetch shows the `prompt` empty-state and needs i18n keys searchPrompt/searchPromptHint.

chart.description: list only — a live chart above the list, driven by the loaded items. Systemic/reusable.

type.description: Chart form (bars = magnitude of recent values, heat-coloured).

max.description: How many most-recent items to plot (default 40).

label.description: i18n key for the chart caption.

sort.description: list only — declarative, PERSISTED sort. Renders a segmented control; the first option is the default. Numbers sort numerically, strings locale-aware. The chosen key is remembered across sessions.

label.description: i18n key for the control label.

prompt.description: searchFetch only — the empty-state shown before the user has typed a query (distinct from `empty`, which is 'no results').

text.description: i18n key for the prompt title (defaults to 'searchPrompt').

hint.description: i18n key for the prompt hint (defaults to 'searchPromptHint').

searchKey.description: i18n key for the search placeholder (defaults to 'search').

statusKey.description: i18n key for the status line under the search bar (defaults to 'status'); interpolated with {date, ...meta}.

source.description: If 'fav', the list reads from saved favorites instead of data.items.

banner.description: Dismissible info banner above the list.

titleKey.description: i18n key for bold lead text.

clientFilters.description: Client-side filters applied when their filter key is truthy.

key.description: Filter state key (must match a filters.controls[].key).

sections.description: Optional grouping of list items into labeled sections by a test() filter.

icon.description: Iconify icon for the section header.

label.description: i18n key for the section header.

accent.description: Highlight the section header/count in primary color.

hideBadge.description: Badge key to suppress on cards inside this section.

labelParams.description: Filter state key whose value is interpolated into the label as {cat}.

layout.description: row -> compact rates row; feed -> rich card; grid -> Android-style launcher icon tile (title only, the tile IS the destination); gallery -> catalog showcase: art-forward tile + title + subtitle + badges, drills into the detail (software/game/media catalogues); table -> dense scannable rows with declared `columns` (+ optional heat).

columns.description: table layout — the columns, left→right. Rows drill into detail (or card.href).

field.description: Item field for the cell value.

label.description: i18n key for the column header (optional).

sub.description: Item field for a small secondary line under the value (e.g. a USD conversion under a BTC amount).

heat.description: Item numeric field — colours a left accent bar + tints this cell by magnitude (log-scaled across visible rows). The color-by-strength encoding.

grow.description: Take the remaining width and truncate (e.g. an address column).

lg.description: Prominent value — larger, semibold (for the headline column a row is scanned by).

format.description: Locale-format the field as a date.

image.description: feed layout (OPTIONAL) — item field with an image URL; renders a 16:9 figure atop the card (media/gallery). grid layout — item field with an icon URL painted into the tile.

imageFit.description: image fit (default 'cover' = fill+crop; 'contain' = whole image matted, for art/posters).

href.description: Item field name holding the URL; makes the card a link (feed opens a new tab; grid navigates same-tab). Field must exist on items.

icon.description: grid layout — item field holding an iconify icon name, rendered on the tile when no image.

bg.description: grid layout (OPTIONAL) — item field with the tile background colour (e.g. brand bg).

fg.description: grid layout (OPTIONAL) — item field with the tile glyph colour (e.g. brand fg).

lead.description: row layout — item field shown in the bold left column.

title.description: Item field name for the card title/heading.

subtitle.description: feed layout — item field for the subtitle.

trailing.description: row layout — item field for the right-aligned value.

unit.description: row layout — unit suffix appended to the numerically-formatted trailing value.

trend.description: row layout (OPTIONAL) — item field with a signed % change; rendered green (up) / red (down) under the trailing value.

more.description: feed layout — i18n key for the 'details' call-to-action.

meta.description: feed layout — footer meta: an item field name, OR { field, format } for a locale-aware date. format:'ago' = past relative (needs i18n agoToday/agoYesterday/agoDays/agoWeeks); format:'when' = future absolute+countdown from a timestamp (needs i18n whenPast/whenMin/whenHours/whenDays).

body.description: feed layout — item field for the clamped body text.

badges.description: Badges shown on the card. Each badge is either field-driven (field) or condition-driven (when+label).

key.description: Badge identity; can be targeted by section.hideBadge.

when.description: test() expression; renders label badge when truthy.

field.description: Item field whose value (string or array) is rendered as badge text.

label.description: i18n key for the badge text (used with 'when').

icon.description: Iconify icon, prepended to the badge (works with both 'when' and 'field' badges).

allOf.$comment: gallery is art-forward: the tile IS the recognition — a software/game catalogue you scan by icon. Without one it is a worse feed. Mirrored in packages/runtime/validate.js; the two validators must stay in lockstep.

then.$comment: UX guardrail: a feed card must carry a preview slot (subtitle/body/image) — a title-only feed card is raw. Use layout:'row' for a compact title+value line, or spec.enrich to fetch a body preview.
A grid tile needs an icon (iconify field) or image (icon URL).

empty.description: Empty-state override for a list tab.

text.description: i18n key for the empty title (defaults to 'noResults').

hint.description: i18n key for the empty hint (defaults to 'noResultsHint').

codeField.description: converter only — item field holding the currency/unit code.

rateField.description: converter only — item field holding the numeric rate.

base.description: converter only — base code with implicit rate 1 (e.g. 'UAH').

hero.description: dashboard only — the big glanceable block. Reads data.meta (a flattened current-conditions object).

place.description: OPTIONAL meta field — small location line above the icon (becomes a tappable city picker when top-level filters exist).

icon.description: OPTIONAL meta field holding an iconify icon name (big glyph).

value.description: REQUIRED meta field — the big number.

unit.description: OPTIONAL literal suffix appended to the big value.

caption.description: OPTIONAL meta field — text under the value.

metrics.description: OPTIONAL labeled stat chips (hidden at watch width).

field.description: REQUIRED meta field for the chip value.

unit.description: Literal suffix on the chip value.

label.description: i18n key shown as the chip label.

strip.description: dashboard only (OPTIONAL) — a horizontal scroller reading a meta ARRAY (e.g. hourly).

from.description: REQUIRED meta key holding the array.

label.description: OPTIONAL i18n key for the section header (gets a clock icon).

icon.description: OPTIONAL item field — iconify icon name.

unit.description: OPTIONAL literal suffix on the strip value.

days.description: dashboard only (OPTIONAL) — a vertical list reading data.items.

label.description: OPTIONAL i18n key for the section header (calendar icon).

icon.description: OPTIONAL item field — iconify icon name.

lo.description: OPTIONAL item field — muted secondary value (hidden at watch width).

weekday.description: OPTIONAL — treat the `day` field as a raw ISO date and render it as a locale-aware short weekday (never bake the weekday string in data.js — no locale there).

i18n.description: Locale dictionaries. 'en' is required as the runtime fallback. Each locale maps i18n keys to display strings; interpolation placeholders use {name} syntax.

allOf.$comment: A card tap must never leave the app. With spec.detail the runtime turns the card into an in-app drill-down; without it, card.href renders the card as <a target=_blank> and the user is thrown out to the source before they can read, save or even see the item. So a non-grid card.href REQUIRES spec.detail (put the outbound link in detail.actions). `grid` is exempt — it is the launcher tile, where opening another app IS the point. Mirrored in packages/runtime/validate.js; the two validators must stay in lockstep.

card.body is API prose and arrives in the source language whatever the locale. Either spec.translate lists it, or spec.localized declares the adapter returns the active locale. Scoped to `body` on purpose: titles/names/addresses are identifiers — machine-translating them makes the app worse. Mirrored in packages/runtime/validate.js; the two validators must stay in lockstep.
